title: Recovery on startup
purpose: Make crash recovery complete and automatic. On engine startup, every non-terminal workflow must be replayed from its full history and resumed at its resume point, with each workflow's Recorder initialised at the correct head so post-recovery appends continue cleanly. A failure recovering one workflow (e.g. a non-determinism violation) must be reported without silently dropping that workflow or aborting recovery of the others.
task: Implement the recovery routine (recovery.rs). It calls EventStore::list_active and, for each WorkflowId, reads its history, builds a Recorder via resume_at at the head implied by that history, and replays the workflow to its resume point (delegating to AD-008's replay). It collects per-workflow outcomes — recovered-to-resume-point, recovered-to-terminal, or failed — so one bad workflow cannot abort the rest. Test by seeding InMemoryStore with multiple active and terminal workflows (including one with a divergent history) and asserting recover handles exactly the active ones correctly and reports the failure for the bad one.

requirements.title: List-active and per-workflow replay
requirements.spec: WHEN recover is invoked, THE SYSTEM SHALL call EventStore::list_active and, for each returned WorkflowId, read its history and replay it to its resume point.
acceptance: recovery.rs defines recover taking the store and LiveExecutor handles.
It calls list_active, then for each id calls read_history and drives AD-008 replay to the resume point.
Terminal workflows are not returned by list_active (per AC contract) and so are not replayed; recover does not re-derive activeness itself.
A unit test with a store stub returning two active ids asserts both are read and replayed.

requirements.title: Recorder initialised at the existing head
requirements.spec: WHILE recovering a workflow, THE SYSTEM SHALL initialise its Recorder at the head implied by its existing recorded history (via Recorder::resume_at) so that appends after recovery continue at the correct sequence.
acceptance: For a workflow with K recorded events, recover constructs its Recorder at head K.
A test recovers a workflow with K events, then drives one live step post-recovery and asserts the new event lands at sequence K (no SequenceConflict).
The head is derived from the read history length/last sequence, not guessed.

requirements.title: Per-workflow failure isolation
requirements.spec: IF replaying one workflow during recovery fails (e.g. a NonDeterminismError or a HistoryShape error), THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL record/report that workflow's failure and SHALL continue recovering the remaining workflows; it SHALL NOT abort the whole recovery or silently drop the failed workflow.
acceptance: recover returns a per-workflow outcome report (e.g. Vec of {WorkflowId, RecoveryOutcome}) where RecoveryOutcome is Resumed{resume_point} | Terminal | Failed(DurabilityError).

A workflow that diverges on replay yields Failed(NonDeterminismError) in the report and the workflow is failed deterministically per AD-005 (one WorkflowFailed recorded), while other workflows still recover.
A test seeds three workflows — two clean active, one divergent — and asserts the report contains two Resumed and one Failed, and that the two clean ones reached their resume points.
recover itself returns Ok with the report for partial-failure cases; it returns Err only for a store-level failure that prevents listing at all.

requirements.title: Recovery test with mixed workflow set
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a test seeding InMemoryStore with multiple active and terminal workflows and asserting recover replays exactly the active ones to their correct resume points.
acceptance: A test seeds InMemoryStore with two active workflows (partial histories with distinct resume points) and one terminal workflow (ending in WorkflowCompleted).
recover is invoked and the report shows the two active workflows Resumed at their correct resume points and does not include the terminal workflow as Resumed.
The test is a real runtime test (not #[ignore], not env-gated) and passes under cargo test.
Post-recovery, a live step on one recovered workflow appends at the correct next sequence (ties R2 into an end-to-end assertion).

boundaries: SHALL NOT spawn the BEAM workflow processes or wire into the engine's supervision tree — AE invokes recover and owns process creation.
SHALL NOT re-implement active-vs-terminal classification — it trusts EventStore::list_active (AC contract).
SHALL NOT execute live timers/signals during recovery beyond the resume-point handoff — live behaviour is AT/AE post-resume.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes (recovery tests over InMemoryStore).
A test proves one divergent workflow does not abort recovery of the others (S8).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
